sentence.text: Except as required by , building frames shall be anchored to the foundation unless a structural analysis that considers wind and earthquake loads and lateral earth pressures shows that anchorage is not required.
Except as provided in , anchorage shall be provided by

clause.text: embedding the ends of the first floor joists in concrete, or
fastening the sill plate to the foundation with not less than 12.7 mm diam anchor bolts spaced not more than 2.4 m o.c.

sentence.text: Except as provided in , where the seismic design parameter, Smax, for Site Class C is greater than 0.47, anchorage of braced wall panel shall be provided by fastening the sill plate to the foundation with anchor bolts, such that

clause.text: there are not less than two anchor bolts per braced wall panel, located at opposite ends of the braced wall panel within 0.5 m of the foundation end or within 0.3 m of the end of the braced wall panel, and
anchor bolts spaced in accordance with . (See .)

clause.text: fastened to the sill plate with nuts and washers,
embedded not less than 100 mm in the foundation, and
so designed that they may be tightened without withdrawing them from the foundation.

sentence.text: Anchorage shall be designed according to Part 4, where

clause.text: the 1-in-50-year hourly wind pressure (HWP) is greater than 1.2 kPa,
the seismic design parameter, Smax, is greater than 2.6, or

clad at full height with masonry veneer, or
clad at full height with stone veneer.

sentence.text: Joints in the top plates of loadbearing walls shall be staggered not less than

clause.text: one stud spacing where the number of nails required by is not more than 16,
two stud spacings where the number of nails required by is greater than 16 and not more than 32, and
three stud spacings where the number of nails required by is greater than 32.

sentence.text: The top plates in loadbearing walls shall be lapped or otherwise tied at corners and intersecting walls in accordance with .
Joints in single top plates used with loadbearing walls shall be tied in accordance with .
Ties referred to in shall be the equivalent of not less than 75 mm by 150 mm by 0.91 mm thick galvanized steel nailed to each wall with at least three 63 mm nails.

Except as provided in , doubled top plates in braced wall bands shall be fastened on each side of a splice with not less than 76 mm long common steel wire nails or spiral nails in accordance with the minimum number of nails required by or , whichever is greater, where

clause.text: the seismic design parameter, Smax, for Site Class C is greater than 0.47 and Smax is not greater than 2.6, or
the 1-in-50-year hourly wind pressure (HWP) is equal to or greater than 0.6 kPa but not greater than 1.2 kPa.

clause.text: the 1-in-50-year hourly wind pressure (HWP) is greater than 1.2 kPa,
the seismic design parameter, Smax, is greater than 2.6, or

amendment.description: Change note reference in Article 9.23.12.2.(1)

new-content.text: Openings in loadbearing walls greater than the required stud spacing shall be framed with lintels designed to carry the superimposed loads to adjacent studs. (See Note A-9.23.10.6.(3).)

amendment.description: Replace entire Article 9.23.13.1 with BC version using S_max and HWP parameters

article.title: Requirements for Low to Moderate Wind and Seismic Forces

sentence.text: This Article applies where

clause.text: the seismic design parameter, Smax, for Site Class C, is not more than 0.47,
the 1-in-50-year hourly wind pressure (HWP) is not greater than 0.60 kPa,
the unsupported height of the braced wall panels in the building is not greater than 3.1 m, and
the lowest exterior frame wall supports a roof and not more than 2 floors.

sentence.text: Bracing to resist lateral loads shall be designed and constructed in accordance with

clause.text: the simplified approach outlined in , where the seismic design parameter, Smax, is not greater than 0.47 and the 1-in-50-year hourly wind pressure (HWP) is not greater than 0.60 kPa,
Part 4, or
good engineering practice such as that provided in .

amendment.description: Replace entire Article 9.23.13.2 with BC version including weight specifications

article.title: Requirements for High Wind and Seismic Forces

sentence.text: Except as provided in , this Article applies where

clause.text: the unsupported height of the braced wall panels in the building is not greater than 3.1 m,
1-in-50-year hourly wind pressure (HWP) is not greater than 1.2 kPa,
the seismic design parameter, Smax, is not greater than 2.6, and
the lowest exterior frame wall supports a roof and not more than

subclause.text: 2 floors in a building of normal-weight construction, or
1 floor in a building of heavyweight construction or clad at full height with masonry veneer or stone veneer.

sentence.text: Bracing to resist lateral loads shall be designed and constructed in accordance with

clause.text: Part 4, or
good engineering practice such as that provided in .

sentence.text: For the purposes of and this Part,

clause.text: in a building of normal-weight construction, the average dead weight per storey shall not exceed

subclause.text: 0.5 kPa for floors and 0.5 kPa for partitions and interior walls,
0.5 kPa for the roof, and
0.4 kPa for exterior walls,

clause.text: in a building of heavyweight construction, the average dead weight per storey shall conform to , except that the average dead weight per storey shall not exceed

subclause.text: 1.5 kPa for floors and 0.5 kPa for partitions and interior walls,
1.0 kPa for the roof, or
1.2 kPa for exterior walls,

clause.text: in a building clad with masonry veneer, the average dead weight of the masonry veneer shall not exceed 1.9 kPa, and
in a building clad with stone veneer, the average dead weight of the stone veneer shall not exceed 3.2 kPa. (See Note A-9.23.13.2.(3).)

amendment.description: Replace entire Article 9.23.13.3 with BC version using S_max and HWP parameters

article.title: Requirements for Extreme Wind and Seismic Forces

sentence.text: Except as provided in , this Article applies where

clause.text: the 1-in-50-year hourly wind pressure (HWP) is greater than 1.2 kPa,
the seismic design parameter, Smax, is greater than 2.6, or

the seismic design parameter, Smax, for Site Class C is greater than 0.47, and the lowest exterior frame wall supports a roof and more than 1 floor in a building of heavyweight construction or clad at full height with masonry veneer or stone veneer.

sentence.text: Bracing to resist lateral loads shall be designed and constructed in accordance with

clause.text: Part 4, or
good engineering practice such as that provided in the .

amendment.description: Replace entire Article 9.23.13.4 with BC version including perimeter requirement

sentence.text: Braced wall bands shall

clause.text: surround the perimeter of the building,
be full storey height,
be not more than 1.2 m wide,
lap at both ends with another braced wall band,
be aligned with braced wall bands on storeys above and below, and
conform to the spacing and dimensions given in and .

sentence.text: For split-level buildings, a braced wall band shall be located where there is a change in floor level greater than the depth of one floor joist.

amendment.description: Replace entire Article 9.23.13.5 with BC version including lateral support, simplified table with notes, and note references

article.title: Braced Wall Panels in Braced Wall Bands

sentence.text: Except as provided in and and , braced wall panels shall

clause.text: be located within braced wall bands,
be laterally supported at each floor level and the roof,
extend, as applicable, from the top of the supporting footing, slab or subfloor to the underside of the floor, ceiling or roof framing above, and
conform to the spacing and dimensions given in and .

table.title: Spacing and Dimensions of Braced Wall Bands and Braced Wall Panels

row.entry: Spacing and Dimensions of Braced Wall Bands and Braced Wall Panels
Maximum distance between centre lines of adjacent braced wall bands measured from the furthest points between centres of the bands
Maximum distance between required braced wall panels measured from the edges of the panels
Maximum distance from the end of a braced wall band to the edge of the closest required braced wall panel
Minimum length of individual wood-sheathed braced wall panels:
• panel located at the end of a braced wall band where the braced wall panel connects to an intersecting braced wall panel
• panel not located at the end of a braced wall band or braced wall panel located at the end of a braced wall band where the braced wall panel does not connect to an intersecting braced wall panel
Minimum length of individual braced wall panels sheathed only with gypsum board
Minimum length of individual diagonal-lumber-sheathed braced wall panels
Minimum total length of all braced wall panels in a braced wall band

note.text: All constructions include support of a roof load in addition to the indicated number of floors.
See for an exception for basements and crawl spaces.

sentence.text: In basements or crawl spaces where the perimeter foundation walls extend from the footings to the underside of the supported floor, braced wall bands constructed with braced wall panels shall

clause.text: have a total length of braced wall panels not less than the total length in the braced wall band in the storey above, and
be spaced not more than

subclause.text: 15 m from the perimeter foundation walls,
15 m from interior foundation walls, and
15 m from adjacent braced wall bands constructed with braced wall panels.

sentence.text: Interior or exterior wood-sheathed braced wall panels, other than panels of WSP-A framing in the uppermost storey shall

clause.text: extend to the roof framing, and
have their the top plate connected to

subclause.text: top chords of perpendicular or offset parallel trusses by using blocking panels or other methods of lateral load transfer designed by the roof truss manufacturer,
perpendicular or offset parallel joists or rafters by using blocking of the same construction as the braced wall panel below, or
rafters, joists or trusses by using methods of lateral load transfer designed in accordance with good engineering practice.

sentence.text: The top plates of braced wall panels described in shall be fastened in accordance with .

amendment.description: Replace entire Article 9.23.13.6 with BC version including simplified requirements and note references

sentence.text: Required braced wall panels shall be

clause.text: sheathed on the exterior side with plywood, OSB, waferboard or diagonal lumber complying with fastened in accordance with and finished on the interior side with gypsum board complying with , or
sheathed on the interior side or exterior side with gypsum board complying with and fastened in accordance with . (See Note A-9.23.13.6.(1).)

sentence.text: Except as provided in and , braced wall bands shall be constructed of braced wall panels of the same sheathing material.
Braced wall panels in basements and crawl spaces shall be sheathed with OSB, plywood, waferboard or diagonal lumber

clause.text: at braced wall band spacing intervals of not more than 15 m, and
under all interior braced wall bands containing wood-sheathed braced wall panels. (See Note A-9.23.13.6.(3).)

sentence.text: Mixing of braced wall panel framing types is permitted in stacked braced wall bands, provided that wood-sheathed braced wall panels are not above any braced wall bands containing

clause.text: gypsum-sheathed braced wall panels, or
diagonal-lumber-sheathed braced wall panels.

sentence.text: Mixing of braced wall panel framing types is permitted along a braced wall band within the same storey, provided that

clause.text: panels of WSP-A or WSP-B framing type are substituted for panels of a GWB framing type and the total length of all of the braced wall panels is determined based on the GWB framing type, or
the lengths of the braced wall panels of mixed framing types are based on accepted engineering principles.

amendment.description: Replace entire Article 9.23.13.7 with BC version on Braced Wall Panel Length calculations

sentence.text: Except as provided in Tables 9.23.13.7.-B and 9.23.13.7.-D, all adjustment factors required for the calculation of the minimum total length of braced wall panels in accordance with this Article shall be taken as 1.

The minimum total length of all braced wall panels in a braced wall band shall be taken as the greater of Lw determined in for the appropriate 1-in-50-year hourly wind pressure (HWP) and Ls as determined in for the appropriate seismic design parameter, Smax, where

clause.text: HWP is not greater than 1.2 kPa, and
Smax for Site Class C, is not greater than 2.6.

sentence.text: For resistance to wind pressure, the minimum total length of braced wall panels in each braced wall band, Lw, shall be determined by applying the adjustment factors provided in Table 9.23.13.7.-B to the unadjusted minimum total braced wall panel length Luw provided in Table 9.23.13.7.-A using the following equation:
Lw = LuwKexpKroofKWspacingKWnumberKgypKsheath ≥ BWPmin
where
Kexp = wind exposure adjustment factor, and = 1 for rough terrain (suburban, urban or wooded terrain extending upwind from the building uninterrupted for at least 1 km),
Kroof = roof eave-to-ridge height adjustment factor, and = 1 for a roof eave-to-ridge height of 3 m
KWspacing = braced wall band spacing adjustment factor for wind (see ),per building plan direction, and = 1 for a braced wall band spacing of 7.6 m
KWnumber = number of parallel braced wall bands adjustment factor for wind, per building plan direction, and = 1 for two exterior walls and no intermediate braced wall bands,
Kgyp = interior gypsum board adjustment factor, and = 1 for braced wall panels with gypsum board installed on the interior side,
Ksheath = intermittent braced wall panels adjustment factor, and = 1 for continuously sheathed braced wall bands, and
BWPmin = Minimum length of individual braced wall panels as per .
(See Note A-9.23.13.7.(3) for an alternative procedure to calculate Lw, directly and Note A-9.23.13.7.(4).)

amendment.description: Insert new Table 9.23.13.7.-A for unadjusted minimum total braced wall panel lengths for wind

table.title: Unadjusted Minimum Total Braced Wall Panel Lengths for Wind

row.entry: Unadjusted Minimum Total Braced Wall Panel Length for Wind, Luw, m (1)
Diagonal- Lumber-Sheathed Framing Type (with gypsum board on opposite side) (2)
Gypsum-Sheathed Framing Type (with gypsum board on only one side) (2) (3)
Wood-sheathed Framing Type ( with gypsum board on opposite side) (2)

table-notes.note: Notes to Table 9.23.13.7.-A:
(1) Unadjusted minimum total braced wall panel lengths are for the applicable conditions corresponding to an adjustment factor of 1 in the equation for Lw.
(2) See Sentence 9.23.3.5.(3) for a description of framing types and fastening requirements.
(3) See Sentence (6) for braced wall panels with gypsum board installed on both sides.

amendment.description: Insert new Table 9.23.13.7.-B for adjustment factors for wind braced wall panel length determination

table.title: Adjustment Factors for the Determination of Minimum Total Braced Wall Panel Lengths for Wind

row.entry: Wind exposure: apply factor to all storeys in both directions
Roof eave-to-ridge height: apply factor separately to each storey
Braced wall band spacing: apply factor to all braced wall panels per building plan direction
Number of parallel braced wall bands: apply factor to all braced wall panels per building plan direction
Interior gypsum board: apply factor in accordance with whether gypsum board is installed or omitted on interior side of braced wall panels
Intermittent braced wall panels: apply factor in accordance with continuity of sheathing within braced wall band

table-notes.note: Notes to Table 9.23.13.7.-B:

(1) Kexp is determined based on the terrain. Rough terrain is suburban, urban, or wooded terrain extending upwind from the building uninterrupted for at least 1 km. Open terrain is level terrain with only scattered trees, buildings or other obstructions, open water or shorelines.
(2) For Kroof linear interpolation between roof eave-to-ridge heights is permitted.
(3) KWspacing Linear interpolation between braced wall band spacing is permitted.
(4) An average braced wall band spacing is permitted to be used for the determination of KWspacing. See Sentence (5).
(5) A braced wall band spacing of 15 m is only permitted in basements and crawl spaces.

amendment.description: Add sentence (4) for seismic forces calculations to Article 9.23.13.7

sentence.text: For resistance to seismic forces, the minimum total length of braced wall panels in each braced wall band, Ls, shall be determined by applying the adjustment factors provided in Table 9.23.13.7.-D to the unadjusted minimum total braced wall panel length, Lus, provided in Table 9.23.13.7.-C using the following equation:
Ls = LusKweightKsnowKSspacingKSnumberKgypKsheath ≥ BWPmin
where
Kweight = weight of construction and cladding adjustment factor, and = 1 for normal-weight construction (see ),
Ksnow = roof snow load adjustment factor, and = 1 for a specified roof snow load of 2 kPa or less, as calculated in accordance with ,
KSspacing = braced wall band spacing adjustment factor for seismic forces (see ), per building plan direction, and = 1 for a braced wall band spacing of 7.6 m,
KSnumber = number of parallel braced wall bands adjustment factor for seismic forces, per building plan direction, and = 1 for two exterior walls and no intermediate braced wall bands,
Kgyp = interior gypsum board adjustment factor, and = 1 for braced wall panels with gypsum board installed on the interior side,
Ksheath = intermittent braced wall panels adjustment factor, and = 1 for continuously sheathed braced wall bands, and
BWPmin = minimum length of individual braced wall panels, per . (See Note A-9.23.13.7.(4) for an alternative procedure to calculate Ls directly and Note A-9.23.13.7.(3) and (4).)

amendment.description: Insert new Table 9.23.13.7.-C for unadjusted minimum total braced wall panel lengths for seismic forces

table.title: Unadjusted Minimum Total Braced Wall Panel Lengths for Seismic Forces

row.entry: Building Plan Dimension Parallel to Braced Wall Band, Lwl, m
Unadjusted Minimum Total Braced Wall Panel Length for Seismic Forces, Lus, m(1)(2)
Diagonal-Lumber-Sheathed Framing Type (with gypsum board on opposite side)(3)
Gypsum-Sheathed Framing Type (with gypsum board on only one side)(3)(4)
Wood-Sheathed Framing Type (with gypsum board on opposite side)(3)

table-notes.note: Notes to Table 9.23.13.7.-C:
(1) Unadjusted minimum total braced wall panel lengths are for the applicable conditions corresponding to an adjustment factor of 1 in the equation for Ls.

(2) DR = design required, using the procedure outlined in Note A-9.23.13.7.(4) or according to Part 4, for braced wall panels with typical sheathing. Lus values within round brackets, to which the reduction set out in Sentence (6) has been applied, are permitted for braced wall panels with gypsum board installed on both sides.
(3) See Sentence 9.23.3.5.(3) for a description of framing types and fastening requirements.
(4) See Sentence (6) for braced wall panels with gypsum board installed on both sides.

amendment.description: Add sentences (5) and (6) for spacing and gypsum board provisions to Article 9.23.13.7

sentence.text: For 3 or more parallel braced wall bands that are not evenly spaced, an average braced wall band spacing is permitted to be used for the determination of KWspacing or KSspacing, provided that no single braced wall band spacing exceeds 10.6 m, except as provided in .

Where braced wall panels of a gypsum-sheathed framing type have gypsum board installed on both sides, the minimum total length of the braced wall panels determined in is permitted to be reduced by 50%.

amendment.description: Insert new Table 9.23.13.7.-D for adjustment factors for seismic braced wall panel lengths

table.title: Adjustment Factors for the Determination of Minimum Total Braced Wall Panel Lengths for Seismic Forces

row.entry: Heavy construction: apply factor corresponding to Lwl separately to each storey
Masonry veneer half storey above foundation: apply factor corresponding to one building face (or two building faces)
Storey supporting roof and up to 2 floors
Masonry veneer cladding perpendicular to braced wall band, fully clad: (3) apply factor corresponding to Lwl for one building face (or two building faces)
Masonry veneer cladding perpendicular to braced wall band, partially clad: (3) apply factor corresponding to Lwl for one building face (or two building faces)
Masonry veneer cladding perpendicular to the braced wall band, 2- storey height, partially clad: (3) apply factor corresponding to Lwl for one building face( or two building faces))
Masonry veneer cladding perpendicular to the braced wall band, 1- storey height, partially clad: (3) apply factor corresponding to Lwl for one building face (or two building faces))

Masonry veneer cladding perpendicular to the braced wall band, 2- storey height, fully clad: Apply factor corresponding to building depth Lwl in the direction of the braced wall band for one or two building face(s)

Masonry veneer cladding perpendicular to the braced wall band, 1- storey height, fully clad: Apply factor corresponding to building depth Lwl in the direction of the braced wall band for one or two building face(s)
Stone veneer half storey above foundation
Supporting up to a roof + 2 floors
Stone veneer cladding perpendicular to braced wall band, fully clad (4) : Apply factor corresponding to building depth in the direction of the braced wall band for one or two building face(s)
Stone veneer cladding perpendicular to braced wall band, partially clad(4): Apply factor corresponding to building depth in the direction of the braced wall bad for one or two building face(s)

Stone veneer cladding perpendicular to the braced wall band, 1 storey height, with openings: Apply factor corresonding to depth Lwl in the direction of the braced wall band for one or two building face(s).

Stone veneer cladding perpendicular to the braced wall band, 2 storey height, with openings: Apply factor corresonding to depth Lwl in the direction of the braced wall band for one or two building face(s).

Stone veneer cladding perpendicular to the braced wall band, 1 storey height, fully clad: Apply factor corresonding to depth Lwl in the direction of the braced wall band for one or two building face(s).

Stone veneer cladding perpendicular to the braced wall band, 2 storey height, fully clad: Apply factor corresonding to depth Lwl in the direction of the braced wall band for one or two building face(s).

Stone veneer cladding perpendicular to the braced wall band, 2 storey height, fully clad: Apply factor corresonding to depth Lwl in the direction of the braced wall band for one or two building face(s).
Braced wall band spacing: apply factor to all braced wall panels per building plan direction
Number of parallel braced wall bands: apply factor to all braced wall panels per building plan direction
Interior gypsum board: apply factor in accordance with whether gypsum board is installed or ommitted on interior side of braced wall panels
Intermittent braced wall panels: apply factor in accordance with continuity of sheathing within braced wall band

table-notes.note: Notes to Table 9.23.13.7.-D:
(2) For Kweight, linear interpolation between Lwl values and between fully clad and partially clad veneer conditions is permitted.
(3) "Fully clad" means that there are no openings, and "partially clad" means 50% or less coverage of an elevation.
(4) For Ksnow, linear interpolation between roof snow loads is permitted.
(5) For Kspacing, linear interpolation between braced wall band spacings is permitted.
(6) An average braced wall band spacing is permitted to be used for the determination of KSspacing. See Sentence 9.23.13.2.(3).
(7) A braced wall band spacing of 15 m is only permitted in basements and crawl spaces.

amendment.description: Insert new Article 9.23.13.8 - Foundation Cripple Walls

sentence.text: Except as provided in , foundation cripple walls supporting braced wall panels shall be

clause.text: considered as an additional storey, or
designed in accordance with Part 4.

sentence.text: Where the seismic design parameter, Smax, is less than or equal to 0.60, foundation cripple walls need not comply with , provided they

clause.text: are not more than 1.2 m in height,
are not more than 6 m in length,
are either

subclause.text: framed with solid blocking, or

of the same construction as the braced wall panels of the storey above but sheathed with wood sheathing regardless of the construction, where the length of the cripple wall bracing is equal to the length of the braced wall panels multiplied by an adjustment factor of 1.2, in addition to any adjustments required by , and

clause.text: do not support heavyweight construction, masonry or stone veneer.

sentence.text: Where the seismic design parameter, Smax, is greater than 0.60, foundation cripple walls need not comply with , provided they

clause.text: comply with ,
are not more than 350 mm in height, and
are not more than 5 m length,

sentence.text: Where interior finish, such as gypsum board, is omitted from the interior side of the cripple wall referred to in or , the interior gypsum board adjustment factor described in or shall be applied to the length of the cripple wall bracing.

amendment.description: Insert new Article 9.23.13.9 - Cripple Walls in Stepped Foundations

sentence.text: Cripple walls in stepped foundations need not be braced in accordance with to , provided

clause.text: the lowest floor framing rests directly on a sill plate anchored to a foundation not less than 2.4 m in length within a braced wall band not more than 7.6 m in length,
the top plate of the cripple wall extends not less than 1.2 m along the foundation, and
anchor bolts are located not more than 300 mm and 900 mm from the step in the foundation.

amendment.description: Insert new Article 9.23.13.10 - Additional System Considerations

sentence.text: This Article applies where

clause.text: the seismic design parameter, Smax, is not greater than 1.2, and
the 1-in-50-year hourly wind pressure (HWP) is not greater than 1.2 kPa.

sentence.text: Portions of the perimeter of a single open or enclosed space need not comply with , where

clause.text: the roof of the space projects not more than

subclause.text: 3.5 m from the face of the framing of the nearest parallel braced wall band, and
half the perpendicular plan dimension,

clause.text: that portion of the perimeter structure does not support a floor,
the roof of the space is

subclause.text: integral with the roof of the rest of the building with framing members not more than 400 mm o.c. where roof sheathing edges are not supported on blocking and not more than 600 mm o.c. where roof sheathing edges are supported on blocking securely fastened between framing members, or

constructed with roof framing not more than 400 mm o.c. where roof sheathing edges are not supported on blocking and not more than 600 mm o.c. where roof sheathing edges are supported on blocking securely fastened between framing members, and fastened to the wall framing (see and for balloon framing), and

clause.text: the end-joists or end-rafters for the roof of the space are fastened to a 3-ply, 38 mm × 140 mm built-up column or a 5-ply, 38 mm × 89 mm built-up column that is integral with the wall framing.

sentence.text: Walls in detached garages and in accessory buildings serving a single dwelling unit, and the front wall of attached garages serving a single dwelling unit need not comply with where these walls do not support a floor.
Braced wall panels in the braced wall band at the front of an attached garage serving a single dwelling unit need not comply with , provided

clause.text: the maximum spacing between the front of the garage and the back wall of the garage does not exceed 7.6 m,
there is not more than one floor above the garage,
not less than 50% of the length of the back wall of the garage is constructed of wood-sheathed braced wall panels, and
not less than 25% of the length of the side walls is constructed of wood-sheathed braced wall panels.

sentence.text: Except as provided in , one exterior wall of the uppermost storey in each orthogonal direction may be set back from the exterior wall of the storey below, provided the adjacent interior braced wall band of the storey below the setback

clause.text: is spaced not more than 10.6 m from the exterior wall of the storey below the setback wall,
consists of braced wall panels that are constructed of a wood-based material in conformance with ,
extends to the foundation, and
is not taken into consideration when providing braced wall panels constructed of a wood-based material at spacing intervals of not more than 15 m as per .

sentence.text: Where the exterior wall of the uppermost storey is set back from the exterior wall of the storey below, the roof and floor space supporting the setback wall shall be sheathed with a wood-based material between the exterior wall of the storey below the setback and the adjacent interior braced wall bands of the storey below the setback.
Where the exterior wall of the uppermost storey is set back from the exterior wall of the storey below, the exterior walls perpendicular to the setback wall shall

clause.text: have their top plate connected with nails that are spaced at no greater than half the spacing required in , and
have their top plate splices fastened with twice the number of nails specified in .

sentence.text: The maximum distance between adjacent required braced wall panels in a braced wall band, measured from the edge of the panels, may be increased to 7.3 m provided that, throughout the height of the building, the length of any braced wall panel within the braced wall band is not less than 1.2 m.

sentence.text: This Article applies to buildings where

the building is located in rough terrain, as described in Note A-9.23.13.7.(3) and (4),
the greatest eave-to-ridge height of the roof is not greater than 3 m,
the braced wall panels are constructed with gypsum board on at least one side,
the braced wall bands are continuously sheathed, and
the building is of normal-weight construction, as defined in , except as provided in .

a house with a secondary suite including their common spaces.

Systems used for ventilation shall conform to the energy efficiency requirements in

sentence.text: Every dwelling unit shall incorporate

clause.text: provisions for non-heating-season ventilation in accordance with , and
except as required by , if supplied with electrical power, provisions for heating-season ventilation in accordance with .

In houses that contain a secondary suite including their common spaces, heating-season ventilation need not be provided for

public corridors, and
ancillary spaces that are not within a dwelling unit, except as provided in .

sentence.text: Rooms or spaces in dwelling units shall be ventilated during the non-heating season by

clause.text: natural ventilation in accordance with , or
a mechanical ventilation system in accordance with

sentence.text: Where a habitable room or space is not provided with natural ventilation as described in , mechanical ventilation shall be provided to exhaust inside air from, or to introduce outside air to, that room or space at the rate of

clause.text: one-half air change per hour if the room or space is mechanically cooled during the non-heating season, or
one air change per hour if the room or space is not mechanically cooled during the non-heating season.

clause.text: , "Residential Mechanical Ventilation Systems,"
this Subsection, or

for ducted mechanical ventilation systems serving more than one dwelling unit in a house with a secondary suite including their common spaces, the mechanical ventilation system shall comply with this Subsection or Part 6.

amendment.description: Replace design and installation requirements with BC-specific provisions in Article 9.32.3.2

sentence.text: Aspects of mechanical ventilation systems not specifically described in this Subsection shall be designed, constructed and installed in accordance with good practice such as that described in the ASHRAE Handbooks and Standards, the the HRAI Residential Mechanical Ventilation Manual, the Hydronics Institute Manuals and the SMACNA Manuals.
Exhaust fans and supply fans shall be installed in accordance with this Subsection and the manufacturer's instructions.
The mechanical components of a mechanical ventilation system shall be installed so as to be accessible for inspection, maintenance, repair, and cleaning.

In a house with a secondary suite including their common spaces, where a heating or ventilation system serves more than a single dwelling unit, the system shall be designed and installed to prevent the circulation of smoke upon a signal from a duct-type smoke detector. (See Note A-9.32.3.2.(4).)
Except as provided in , ducts penetrating fire separations shall be equipped with fire dampers in conformance with .

sentence.text: A mechanical ventilation system shall include:

clause.text: a principal ventilation fan system that

subclause.text: provides supply air in accordance with , and
includes an exhaust fan that conforms with ,

clause.text: the kitchen and bathroom exhaust fans that are required by , and
if the building includes a heated crawl space, the components that are required by .

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.32.3.4 with BC-specific ventilation system supply air requirements

sentence.text: Except as provided in , a principal ventilation system shall mechanically provide supply air in accordance with , , or .
Where the principal ventilation system is a ducted forced-air heating system, the ducted forced-air heating system shall

clause.text: provide supply air through the ducting to

subclause.text: each bedroom,
each floor level without a bedroom, and

ancillary spaces that contain an exhaust device, where the space is not within a dwelling unit in a house with a secondary suite and where the house with a secondary suite contains a fuel-fired space-heating appliance or fuel-fired water-heating appliance of other than direct-vented or mechanically vented types,

clause.text: draw supply air from an outdoor inlet that is connected to the cabinet containing the furnace air circulating fan required by by ducting that measures, from that cabinet to the point at which the ducting intersects the return air plenum,

subclause.text: between 3 m and 4.5 m in length, or
if a flow control device is used, not more than 4.5 m in length,

clause.text: draw supply air through ducting that is

subclause.text: rigid ducting with an equivalent diameter of at least 100 mm, or
flexible ducting with an equivalent diameter of at least 125 mm, and

clause.text: have a furnace air circulating fan set to run continuously.

sentence.text: Where the principal ventilation system is a ducted forced-air heating system used in combination with a heat-recovery ventilator,

clause.text: the ducted forced-air heating system shall conform to , and ,
the heat-recovery ventilator shall draw supply air from an outdoor inlet into the return air plenum of the ducted forced-air heating system, and
the heat-recovery ventilator shall draw exhaust air, through dedicated ducting,

subclause.text: from one or more indoor inlets, at least one of which is located at least 2 m above the floor of the uppermost floor level, and
at the capacity rating of the heat-recovery ventilator, which shall be no less than the air-flow rate specified in .

sentence.text: Where the principal ventilation system is a heat-recovery ventilator, the heat-recovery ventilator shall

clause.text: provide supply air through dedicated ducting to

subclause.text: each bedroom,
each floor level without a bedroom, and
each ancillary space described in , and

clause.text: draw exhaust air, through dedicated ducting,

subclause.text: from one or more indoor inlets, at least one of which is located at least 2 m above the floor of the uppermost floor level, and
at the capacity rating of the heat-recovery ventilator, which shall be no less than the air-flow rate specified in .

sentence.text: Where the principal ventilation system is a ducted central-recirculation ventilation system, the ducted central-recirculation ventilation system shall

clause.text: draw supply air from an outdoor inlet connected upstream of the fan,
draw air from

subclause.text: each bedroom and deliver it to a common area, or
a common area and deliver it to each bedroom, and

clause.text: deliver air to each ancillary space described in .

sentence.text: A principal ventilation system need not conform to if the principal ventilation system

clause.text: services a dwelling unit that

subclause.text: is located where the January design temperature, on a 2.5% basis determined in conformance with , is greater than −20°C,
has only 1 storey and a floor area of less than 168 m2 within the building envelope,
does not have a ducted forced-air heating system, and
except for a secondary suite, is not located in a building conforming to or , and

clause.text: provides supply air passively from outdoors through dedicated inlets that

subclause.text: are located in each bedroom, at least one common area and each ancillary space described in ,
are located at least 1 800 mm above the floor, and
have an unobstructed vent area of not less than 25 cm2 .

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.32.3.5 with BC-specific principal ventilation system exhaust fan requirements

sentence.text: A principal ventilation system exhaust fan shall

clause.text: run continuously, and
provide at least the air-flow rate specified in .

table.title: Principal Ventilation System Exhaust Fan Minimum Air-flow Rate

sentence.text: For the purposes of , the capacity rating of the principal ventilation system exhaust fan shall be determined, based on air-flow performance at 50 pa of external static pressure, in accordance with

clause.text: HVI Publication 916, "Airflow Test Procedure," or
CAN/CSA-C260-M, "Rating the Performance of Residential Mechanical Ventilating Equipment."

sentence.text: The principal ventilation system exhaust fan shall be

clause.text: designed to run continuously, and
controlled by a dedicated switch that

subclause.text: has 2 settings, on and off,
is located where it will be accessible for the purposes of servicing the exhaust fan but not likely to be turned off inadvertently, and
is clearly marked "PRINCIPAL VENTILATION EXHAUST FAN."

sentence.text: If the principal ventilation system exhaust fan is designed to run at multiple air-flow rates,

clause.text: the air-flow rate of the fan shall be controlled by a switch other than the switch described in , and
the lowest air-flow rate shall not be less than the air-flow rate specified in .

sentence.text: The sound rating of the principal ventilation system exhaust fan shall not exceed 1.0 sone when running continuously at the air-flow rate specified in as determined in accordance with

clause.text: HVI Publication 915, "Loudness Testing and Rating Procedure," or
CAN/CSA-C260-M, "Rating the Performance of Residential Mechanical Ventilating Equipment."

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.32.3.6 with BC-specific kitchen and bathroom exhaust fan requirements

sentence.text: An exhaust fan that provides at least the air-flow rate specified in shall be installed in

clause.text: every kitchen, and
every bathroom or water-closet room, unless the bathroom or water-closet room is served by the principal ventilation system exhaust fan that complies with .

sentence.text: For the purposes of , the capacity rating of the exhaust fan shall be determined, based on air-flow performance at 50 pa of external static pressure, in accordance with

clause.text: HVI Publication 916, "Airflow Test Procedure," or
CAN/CSA-C260-M, "Rating the Performance of Residential Mechanical Ventilating Equipment."

table.title: Kitchen/Bathroom Exhaust Fan Minimum Air-flow Rate

row.entry: Minimum Exhaust Fan Air-flow Rate, L/s

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.32.3.7 with BC-specific heated crawl space ventilation requirements

sentence.text: Where a crawl space is heated by a ducted forced-air heating system that does not draw air from the crawl space to the furnace through the return air plenum, the crawl space shall be connected to the floor space above the crawl space by at least one air-transfer grille.
Where a crawl space is heated other than by a ducted forced-air heating system, the crawl space shall

clause.text: be connected to

subclause.text: the floor space above the crawl space by at least one air-transfer grille, and
the principal ventilation system by a supply air outlet or an exhaust air inlet,

clause.text: be connected to the floor space above the crawl space by at least 2 air-transfer grilles for every 30 m2 of crawl space area, or
be connected to

subclause.text: the floor space above the crawl space by at least one air-transfer grille, and
the outdoors by a dedicated exhaust fan that complies with .

sentence.text: An air-transfer grille required by or shall have an unobstructed vent area of the greater of

clause.text: 25 cm2 , and
0.83 cm2 for every m2 of crawl space area.

sentence.text: Where a dedicated exhaust fan is installed in accordance with , the dedicated exhaust fan shall

clause.text: provide an air-flow rate of at least 23 L/s, and
be controlled by

subclause.text: a humidity control device, or
an adjustable time control device that is capable of providing not less than 8 total hours of ventilation per 24 hour period.

sentence.text: Where a crawl space is divided into 2 or more compartments, each heated compartment shall conform to or .

amendment.description: Insert complete Article 9.32.4.2 Carbon Monoxide Alarms with BC-specific content

sentence.text: This Article applies to every building that contains a residential occupancy, a business and personal services occupancy, or a mercantile occupancy and that

clause.text: is served by or contains a fuel-burning appliance, or
contains a storage garage.

sentence.text: Carbon monoxide (CO) alarms installed in a residential occupancy required by this Article shall

clause.text: conform to ,
be equipped with an integral alarm that satisfies the audibility requirements of ,
have no disconnect switch between the overcurrent device and the CO alarm, where the CO alarm is powered by the dwelling unit's electrical system, and
be installed as recommended by the manufacturer.

sentence.text: Where a room in a residential occupancy contains a solid-fuel-burning appliance, a CO alarm conforming to shall be

clause.text: installed as recommended by the manufacturer where these instructions specifically mention solid-fuel-burning appliances, or
in the absence of specific instructions related to solid-fuel-burning appliances, on or near the ceiling.

sentence.text: Where a fuel-burning appliance is installed in a suite of residential occupancy, a CO alarm shall be installed

clause.text: inside each bedroom, or
outside each bedroom, within 5 m of each bedroom door, measured following corridors and doorways.

sentence.text: Where a fuel-burning appliance serves a residential occupancy and is installed in a service room that is not in a suite of residential occupancy, a CO alarm shall be installed

clause.text: either inside each bedroom, or if outside, within 5 m of each bedroom door, measured following corridors and doorways, in every suite of residential occupancy that shares a wall or floor/ceiling assembly with the service room, and
in the service room.

sentence.text: For each suite of residential occupancy that shares a wall or floor/ceiling assembly with a storage garage or that is adjacent to an attic or crawl space to which the storage garage is also adjacent, a CO alarm shall be installed

clause.text: inside each bedroom, or
outside each bedroom, within 5 m of each bedroom door, measured following corridors and doorways.

sentence.text: Where CO alarms are installed in a house with a secondary suite including their common spaces, the CO alarms shall be interconnected so that the actuation of any one CO alarm causes all CO alarms within the house with a secondary suite including their common spaces to sound.
CO alarms installed in a business and personal services occupancy, or a mercantile occupancy as required by this Article shall conform to

clause.text: CAN/CSA-6.19, "Residential carbon monoxide alarming devices," notwithstanding the scope of that standard,
UL 2034, "Standard for Single and Multiple Station Carbon Monoxide Alarms," notwithstanding the scope of that standard, or
good engineering practice.

sentence.text: Where a fuel-burning appliance serves a business and personal services occupancy, or mercantile occupancy, a CO alarm shall be,

clause.text: where the fuel-burning appliance is part of a system that could circulate or distribute CO to a suite of business and personal services occupancy or mercantile occupancy, installed on each storey of each suite that may be exposed, and
installed in the room or space in which the fuel-burning appliance is located.

sentence.text: For each suite of business and personal services occupancy or mercantile occupancy that shares a wall or floor/ceiling assembly with either a storage garage, or a service room containing a fuel-burning appliance, or that is adjacent to an attic or crawl space to which either a storage garage, or a service room containing a fuel-burning appliance is also adjacent, a CO alarm shall be installed

clause.text: on each storey of the adjacent suite, and
in each service room containing a fuel-burning appliance.

amendment.description: Remove NBC Article 9.32.3.11 Ducts
Remove NBC Article 9.32.3.12 Heat Recovery Ventilators
Remove NBC Article 9.32.3.13 Outdoor Intake and Exhaust Openings
Remove sentence about linear interpolation from Article 9.32.3.4
Remove Table 9.32.3.4.1 Maximum Outdoor Airflow from Article 9.32.3.4
Modify Article 9.33.1.1 to expand scope and add fire safety conditions

sentence.text: This Section applies to the design and installation of

clause.text: heating systems, including requirements for combustion air, and air-conditioning systems serving only one dwelling unit or houses with a secondary suite including their common spaces, and
reserved.

sentence.text: The design and installation of heating systems, including requirements for combustion air, and air-conditioning systems other than those described in shall conform to . (See and )

Unless the air duct distribution systems serving one of the dwelling units in a house with a secondary suite are designed and installed to prevent the circulation of smoke in accordance with and equipped with fire dampers in accordance with , the air duct distribution system shall not be directly interconnected with other parts of the house.
Systems used for heating and air-conditioning shall conform to the energy efficiency requirements in

amendment.description: Change Subsection 9.33.2 title to include cooling systems
Modify Article 9.33.2.1 to include cooling systems and add cooling requirements

sentence.text: Residential buildings intended for use in the winter months on a continuing basis shall be equipped with heating facilities conforming to this Section.

Except where determination according to or good engineering practice according to can show it to be unnecessary, dwelling units intended for use in the summer months on a continuing basis shall be equipped with cooling facilities conforming to this Section. (See )

amendment.description: Modify Article 9.33.3.1 to fix clause ordering and add cooling temperature requirements

sentence.text: At the outside winter design temperature, required heating facilities shall be capable of maintaining an indoor air temperature of not less than

clause.text: 22°C in all living spaces,
18°C in unfinished basements,
18°C in common service rooms, ancillary spaces and exits in houses with a secondary suite, and
15°C in heated crawl spaces.

sentence.text: At the outside summer design temperature, required cooling facilities shall be capable of maintaining an indoor air temperature of not more than 26°C in at least one living space in each dwelling unit.

amendment.description: Modify Article 9.33.4.1 to simplify scope and update manual references

sentence.text: Aspects of heating and air-conditioning systems not specifically addressed in this Subsection, including ducting, and mechanical heating and refrigeration equipment, shall be designed, constructed and installed in accordance with good practice such as that described in the ASHRAE Handbooks and Standards, the the CHC Handbook on Hydronic Heating Systems, the Hydronics Institute Manuals, the SMACNA Manuals and the TECA Quality First Manuals. (See also for the design of systems that also provide ventilation.)

amendment.description: Simplify Article 9.33.4.2 to reference CSA B214 directly

sentence.text: The installation of a hydronic heating system shall conform to .

amendment.description: Add sentence 2 to Article 9.33.4.4 about secondary suite access requirements

sentence.text: Where a heating or air-conditioning system serves more than one dwelling unit in a house with a secondary suite including their common spaces, access required by from more than one dwelling unit, common space or ancillary space is not required.

amendment.description: Modify Article 9.33.4.7 to use seismic design parameter terminology

new-content.text: Where the building is in a location where the seismic design parameter, Smax, for Site Class C is greater than 0.37, heating and air-conditioning equipment with fuel or power connections shall be secured to the structure to resist overturning and displacement. (See .)

amendment.description: Change Subsection 9.33.5 title from air-conditioning to cooling

find-replace.find: Heating and Air-conditioning Appliances and Equipment
find-replace.replace: Heating and Cooling Appliances and Equipment

amendment.description: Modify Article 9.33.5.1 to include cooling appliances

article.title: Capacity of Heating and Cooling Appliances

sentence.text: The required capacity of heating and cooling appliances located in a dwelling unit and serving only that dwelling unit, shall be determined in accordance with except that the design temperatures shall conform to

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.33.5.2 to simplify installation standards

sentence.text: Except as provided in , the installation of heating and air-conditioning equipment, including mechanical refrigeration equipment, and including provisions for mounting, clearances and air supply, shall conform to

clause.text: the Safety Standards Act and pursuant regulations,
, and

sentence.text: (See also .)

amendment.description: Add sentence 2 to Article 9.33.5.3 about Safety Standards Act equivalency

sentence.text: For the purposes of , solid-fuel-burning boiler appliances that are approved for use under section 10 of the Safety Standards Act satisfy section 4.1 of . (See also Subclause 9.33.5.2.(1)(a)(ii).)

amendment.description: Add full title of CAN/ULC-S109 standard in Article 9.33.6.3

new-content.text: Tape used for sealing duct joints in air ducts, plenums and other parts of air duct systems shall meet the flame-resistance requirements for fabric in , "Standard Method for Flame Tests of Flame-Resistant Fabrics and Films."

amendment.description: Add full title of ASTM C411 standard in Article 9.33.6.4.(4)

new-content.text:
Combustible coverings, linings and foamed plastic insulation described in
shall not flame, glow, smoulder or smoke when tested in accordance with the method of test in , "Standard Specification for Hot-Surface Performance of High-Temperature Thermal Insulation," at the maximum temperature to which they are to be exposed in service.

amendment.description: Add full title of ANSI/SMACNA 006 standard in Article 9.33.6.5.(2)

new-content.text: The design of fittings for ducts shall conform to , "HVAC Duct Construction Standards -- Metal and Flexible," except that metal thicknesses shall conform to

amendment.description: Replace note reference with plain text in Article 9.33.6.8.(2)(b)

new-content.text: be not less than 12 mm at a distance of 450 mm or more from the plenum, except that this clearance may be reduced to zero beyond a bend or offset in the duct sufficiently large to shield the remainder of the supply duct from direct radiation from the furnace heat exchanger. (See Note A-3.6.5.6.(2).)

amendment.description: Replace note reference with plain text in Article 9.33.6.8.(3)(b)

new-content.text: not less than 12 mm at a horizontal distance of 1.8 m or more from the plenum, except that this distance may be reduced to zero beyond a bend or offset in the duct sufficiently large to shield the remainder of the duct from direct radiation from the furnace heat exchanger. (See Note A-3.6.5.6.(3).)

amendment.description: Replace note reference with plain text in Article 9.33.6.8.(4)(c)

new-content.text: not less than 25 mm at a horizontal distance of 1.8 m or more from the plenum, except that this distance may be reduced to 8 mm beyond a bend or offset in the duct sufficiently large to shield the remainder of the supply duct from direct radiation from the furnace heat exchanger. (See Note A-3.6.5.6.(4).)

amendment.description: Change em dash to three hyphens in Article 9.33.6.10 title

find-replace.find: Warm-Air Supply Outlets and Return Inlets — General
find-replace.replace: Warm-Air Supply Outlets and Return Inlets --- General

amendment.description: Add note reference to Article 9.33.6.13
Add full title of ASTM C411 standard in Article 9.33.8.2.(2)(b)

new-content.text: shall not flame, glow, smoulder or smoke when tested in accordance with , "Standard Specification for Hot-Surface Performance of High-Temperature Thermal Insulation," at the maximum temperature to which such insulation or covering is to be exposed in service.

amendment.description: Replace note reference with plain text in Article 9.33.8.2.(5)

new-content.text: Pipes that are exposed to human contact shall be insulated so that the exposed surface does not exceed 70°C. (See Note A-6.5.1.1.(3).)

amendment.description: Add full title of CAN/ULC-S629 standard and replace note reference with plain text in Article 9.33.10.2.(1)

new-content.text: Factory-built chimneys serving solid-fuel-burning appliances, and their installation, shall conform to , "Standard for 650°C Factory-Built Chimneys." (See Note A-9.33.10.2.(1).)

amendment.description: Replace CSA C22.1 reference with Safety Standards Act in Article 9.34.1.1.(1)

new-content.text: Electrical installations, including the service capacity of the installation and the number and distribution of circuits and receptacles, shall meet the requirements of the appropriate provincial, territorial or municipal legislation or, in the absence of such legislation, shall conform to the Safety Standards Act and pursuant regulations

amendment.description: Add new sentence (4) regarding illumination for rooms and spaces used by the public in Article 9.34.2.7

sentence.text: Notwithstanding to , rooms and spaces used by the public shall be equipped to provide illumination as described in to .

amendment.description: Add note reference to Article 9.35.2.2.(1)
Replace Article 9.32.3.8 Protection Against Depressurization with Air Ducts content

sentence.text: Except as required by , this Article applies to air ducts other than those described in .
Exhaust ducts shall discharge to the outdoors.
Exhaust ducts that are downstream of an exhaust fan shall have no connections to other fans or ducts.
Exhaust ducts, and supply ducts that conduct heated or cooled air, shall

clause.text: be sized in accordance with the requirements of the manufacturer of the fans to which they are connected, and
have an equivalent diameter not less than that specified by .

table.title: Maximum Equivalent Duct Length(1), m

row.entry: Equivalent Diameter, mm (Cross Section Area for Rectangular Ducts, cm2)
Equivalent Diameter, mm (Cross Section Area for Rectangular Ducts, cm2)

note.text: The equivalent length of a duct is the length of the duct plus 10 m for the exterior hood and 3 m for each 90° elbow.

sentence.text: Where an exhaust duct passes through or is located adjacent to a space that is not conditioned space, the duct shall conform to , except that in no case shall such a duct be insulated to less than RSI 0.75.
Where a principal ventilation system supply duct passes through or is located adjacent to a conditioned space, the duct shall be

clause.text: insulated to not less than RSI 0.75, and
provided with an effective vapour barrier.

sentence.text: Where a kitchen exhaust fan grille is installed within 1.2 m horizontally of a cooktop, the exhaust fan duct shall

clause.text: be constructed of a material that is noncombustible, corrosion-resistant, and cleanable, and
be equipped with a grease filter at the intake end.

sentence.text: Except for a supply air system described in or , all joints in exhaust ducts, and in supply ducts that conduct conditioned air, shall be sealed against air leakage with

clause.text: sealants or gaskets made from liquids, mastics or heat-applied materials,
mastic with embedded fabric,
foil-faced butyl tape, or
aluminum foil tape.

sentence.text: Supply ducts for a mechanical ventilation system shall not be used to provide combustion or dilution air to fuel-burning appliances.

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.32.3.9 Carbon Monoxide Alarms with Outdoor Inlets and Outlets content

sentence.text: Outdoor air inlets and exhaust outlets shall be shielded from the weather, birds and rodents by using hoods incorporating a screen of corrosion-resistant material with openings of 6 to 12 mm.

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.32.3.10 Fans with Interior Distribution content

sentence.text: Interior doors shall be undercut by a minimum of 12 mm above the finished floor or the rooms shall be provided with an air-transfer grille with an unobstructed vent area that is not less than 100 cm2 .

amendment.description: Insert new BC-specific Subsection 9.32.4 Additional Protection Against Depressurization

sentence.text: Additional make-up air for the actual appliance exhaust rate shall be provided for any appliance that discharges air to the exterior at an installed rate exceeding 0.5 air changes per hour when it is located within a dwelling unit or house with a secondary suite that contains a vented appliance that is subject to back drafting (Naturally Aspirating Fuel Fired Vented Appliance). (See Note A-9.32.4.1.)
Where additional make-up air is required for appliances described in , it shall be provided by a supply fan rated to deliver outdoor air at the rate of the installed exhaust appliance.
The supply fan as required in shall be interconnected with the exhaust fan for which make-up air is required.
The outdoor air required by shall be

clause.text: tempered to at least 1°C before being introduced to a normally unoccupied area of the dwelling unit or house with a secondary suite including their common spaces, or
tempered to at least 12°C before being introduced to occupied areas either by passive transfer grille or directly from outside.

amendment.description: Replace Table 9.34.2.7 to add row for rooms for storage of combustible refuse

table.title: Lighting for Public Areas

row.entry: Minimum Lighting Power Density, W/m2 of floor area (incandescent lighting)
Rooms for storage of combustible refuse

amendment.description: Add note reference to Article 9.36.1.1.(1)
Add new sentence (9) defining log homes in Article 9.36.1.2

sentence.text: For the purposes of this Section, the term "log homes" shall mean homes with a type of construction whereby the exterior vertical opaque walls primarily consist of structural log members.

amendment.description: Reorder clauses in alphabetical order in Article 9.37.1.3.(1)

sentence.text: Except as provided in to , buildings shall comply with

clause.text: the prescriptive or trade-off requirements in to ,
the performance requirements in ,
the tiered performance requirements in ,
the tiered prescriptive requirements in , or
the .

amendment.description: Replace sentence content with BC-specific compliance requirements in Article 9.36.1.3.(3)

sentence.text: Except as permitted in , buildings described in and shall comply with :

clause.text: houses with or without a secondary suite including their common spaces, and

except for common spaces in a house with a secondary suite, buildings containing only dwelling units and common spaces whose total floor area does not exceed 20% of the total floor area of the building.

amendment.description: Replace sentence 6 and add sentences 7-8 with BC-specific requirements in Article 9.37.1.3.(6)-(8)

sentence.text: Notwithstanding and , and except for the purpose of modeling the reference building for compliance with and , Tables 9.37.2.6.-C, 9.37.2.7.-D, and 9.37.2.8.-C apply only to

clause.text: houses with or without a secondary suite including their common spaces, and

except for common spaces in a house with its secondary suite, buildings containing only dwelling units and common spaces whose total floor area does not exceed 20% of the total floor area of the building.

sentence.text: Log homes shall conform to the requirements of

clause.text: to ,
, or

sentence.text: Notwithstanding , Tables 9.37.2.6.-C, 9.37.2.7.-D, and 9.37.2.8.-C do not apply to log homes.

amendment.description: Add clause (c) with Table 9.37.2.6.-C requirements and note reference in Article 9.37.2.6.(1)

sentence.text: Except as provided in and and and , the effective thermal resistance of above-ground opaque building assemblies or portions thereof shall be not less than that shown for the applicable heating-degree day category in

clause.text: , where the ventilation system does not include heat-recovery equipment, or
, where the ventilation system includes heat-recovery equipment conforming to , or
Table 9.37.2.6.-C, where the ventilation or space conditioning system contain either:

subclause.text: a heat-recovery ventilator conforming to , or
a heat pump in conformance with .

sentence.text: (See .)

(3) For the purposes of this table, a roof deck shall mean a horizontal portion of a roof intended for occupancy.
(4) For the purposes of this table, flat roofs shall mean a roof that is not intended for occupancy.
(5) See for requirements concerning the above-ground portion of foundation walls.

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.37.2.7 with BC version containing 11 sentences and new Table D

article.title: Thermal Characteristics of Fenestration, Doors and Skylights

sentence.text: Except as provided in to and , fenestration and doors shall have an overall thermal transmittance (U-value) not greater than the values listed in for the applicable heating-degree day category. (See )

table.title: Required Thermal Characteristics of Fenestration and Doors

row.entry: Heating Degree-Days of Building Location,See . in Celsius Degree-Days
FenestrationExcept skylights and glass block assemblies. and doors

sentence.text: Skylights shall have an overall thermal transmittance not greater than the values listed in for the applicable heating-degree day category. (See )

table.title: Overall Thermal Transmittance of Skylights

row.entry: Heating Degree-Days of Building Location,See . in Celsius Degree-Days

sentence.text: Except for site-assembled or site-glazed factory-made fenestration products, curtain wall construction, and site-built windows and glazed doors that are tested in accordance with , site-built windows and glazed doors need not comply with , provided they are constructed in accordance with one of the options presented in for the applicable climate zone. (See )

table.title: Compliance Options for Site-built Windows and Glazed Portion of Doors

sentence.text: Glass block assemblies separating conditioned space from unconditioned space or the exterior shall have

clause.text: an overall thermal transmittance of not more than 2.9 W/(m2×K), and
a total aggregate area of not more than 1.85 m2.

sentence.text: One door separating a conditioned space from an unconditioned space or the exterior is permitted to have an overall thermal transmittance up to 2.6 W/(m2×K).
Storm windows and doors need not comply with .
Vehicular access doors separating a conditioned space from an unconditioned space or the exterior shall have a nominal thermal resistance of not less than 1.1 (m2×K)/W.
Access hatches separating a conditioned space from an unconditioned space shall be insulated to a nominal thermal resistance of not less than 2.6 (m2×K)/W.
A door separating a conditioned space from an unconditional space or the exterior is not required to conform to if,

clause.text: in the case of a building in a location with a heating degree-day value of less than or equal to 3999, the door is one of not more than three nonconforming doors, each of which has an overall thermal transmittance not greater than 2.10 W/m2·K,

in the case of a building in a location with a heating degree-day value of at least 4000 and not greater than 5999, the door is one of not more than two nonconforming doors, each of which has an overall thermal transmittance not greater than 2.10 W/m2·K,

in the case of a building in a location with a heating degree-day value of greater than or equal to 6000, the door is one of not more than two nonconforming doors, each of which has an overall thermal transmittance not greater than 2.00 W/m2·K, or
in any case, the door is the only nonconforming door and has an overall thermal transmittance not greater than 2.60 W/m2·K.

sentence.text: A building described in or is permitted to have an additional nonconforming door with an overall thermal transmittance not greater than 2.10 W/m2·K if the effective thermal resistance of the ceilings of the building is at least 0.88 m2·K/W greater than the relevant value shown in or , as applicable.
For buildings described in , fenestration shall have an overall thermal transmittance (U-value) not greater than the values listed in for the applicable heating-degree day category.

subclause.text: a heat-recovery ventilator conforming to , or
a heat pump in conformance with .

table-notes.note: Notes to Table 9.36.2.8.-C:
(1) See Note A-Tables 9.36.2.8.-A and -B.
(2) See .
(3) Does not apply to below-grade floors over heated crawl spaces.
(4) Unheated floors shall be insulated to the effective thermal resistance required in Table 9.36.2.8.-C under their full bottom surface including exterior edges.
(5) Typically applies to floors-on-ground in full-height basements.
(6) Refers to undisturbed frost line before house is constructed.
(7) See for requirement on placement of insulation. The design of slabs-on-grade with an integral footing is addressed in Part 4 (see ).

sentence.text: For the purpose of this Subsection, the term "reference house" shall mean a hypothetical replica of the proposed house design using the same energy sources for the same functions and having the same environmental requirements, occupancy, climatic data and operating schedules, but made to comply with all applicable prescriptive requirements of , not including

amendment.description: Replace entire Article 9.37.5.3 with BC-specific compliance requirements and add note reference

sentence.text: The performance compliance calculations shall determine the annual energy consumption of the proposed house and the house energy target of a reference house in accordance with

clause.text: this Subsection, or
the EnerGuide Rating System, version 15, and .

sentence.text: (See .)

Except as permitted by , the annual energy consumption of the proposed house in comparison to the house energy target of the reference house shall demonstrate a percent improvement in accordance with Table 9.37.6.3.-G. (See .)

Except for Tables 9.37.2.6.-C, 9.37.2.7.-D, and 9.37.2.8.-C, in establishing the house energy target, building components, systems and assemblies shall be accounted for in accordance with the prescriptive requirements of Subsections 9.37.2. to 9.37.4. for the climate zone under consideration.

In establishing the annual energy consumption, building components, systems and assemblies that are addressed in the scope of the prescriptive requirements of Subsections 9.37.2. to 9.37.4. shall be accounted for for the climate zone under consideration.

Where the construction techniques or building components, systems or assemblies used are more energy-efficient than those prescribed by the prescriptive requirements, the performance compliance calculations are permitted to take this increased performance level into account in the determination of the annual energy consumption, provided it can be quantified and is not dependent on occupant interaction.
Both the proposed and reference houses shall be modeled using the same climatic data, soil conditions, operating schedules in and temperature set-points.
For log homes, the annual energy consumption of the proposed house shall not exceed the house energy target of the reference house. (See .)

Insert new Section - Greenhouse Gas Emissions

sentence.text: Notwithstanding , the requirements of this Section apply only to buildings that

clause.text: are a house with or without a secondary suite including their common spaces,
contain only houses and common spaces whose total floor area does not exceed 20% of the total floor area of the building, and
are designed to one of the following

subclause.text: Step 3, 4 or 5 in Tables 9.36.6.3.-A to 9.36.6.3.-H, or

sentence.text: Where a building contains more than one house, with or without a secondary suite, the requirements of this Subsection shall apply to the greenhouse gas emissions of the building and not to the individual house. (See Note A-9.36.6.2.(2).)
Notwithstanding , for the purposes of this Section, the term "house" shall mean all houses, with or without a secondary suite, that

clause.text: have HVAC systems that serve only the house, only the secondary suite, or both the house and the secondary suite including their common spaces,
have service water heating systems that serve only the house, only the secondary suite, or both the house and the secondary suite including their common spaces, and
except for common spaces in the house with the secondary suite, do not share common spaces intended for occupancy with other dwelling units or houses.

sentence.text: For the purposes of this Section, the term "greenhouse gas", expressed with the abbreviation "GHG", shall have the same meaning as defined in the Climate Change Accountability Act.

For the purposes of this Section, the term "greenhouse gas intensity", expressed with the abbreviation "GHGI", shall mean the total greenhouse gas emissions, expressed as kgCO2e per square metre of floor area of conditioned space per year, associated with the energy used by the building's systems.

For the purposes of this Section, the symbol "CO2e" shall mean carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide equivalent, with carbon dioxide equivalent meaning one or more greenhouse gases with a global warming potential equivalent to carbon dioxide.
For the purposes of this Section, the term "Emissions Level" shall mean an Emissions Level referred to in Table 9.37.1.3.
For the purposes of this Section, the term "total greenhouse gases" shall mean the total greenhouse gas emissions, expressed as kgCO2e/year, associated with the energy used by the building's systems.

For the purposes of this Section, the term "measure only" shall mean that the GHGI and the total greenhouse gases are calculated, as applicable, and the values reported in accordance with Subsection 2.2.8. of Division C.
Buildings conforming to the requirements of any of GHG Emission Levels EL-1 to EL-4 shall be designed and constructed to conform to one of the GHG emission compliance options in Table 9.37.1.3.

table.title: Greenhouse Gas Emissions

row.entry: Maximum GHG Emissions by House, Expressed in kg CO2e/year
Reduction of GHG Emissions by Energy Source of Building Systems
Maximum GHGI of the House, Expressed in kgCO2e/m2/year
Maximum GHG Emissions by House, Expressed in kgCO2e/year
Energy sources supplying heating systems have an emissions factor ≤ 0.011 kgCO2e/kWh
Energy sources supplying heating and service water heating systems have an emissions factor ≤ 0.011 kgCO2e/kWh
Energy sources supplying all building systems, including equipment and appliances, have an emissions factor ≤ 0.011 kgCO2e/kWh

note.text: Compliance for this option is demonstrated by meeting both GHGI and GHG emission requirements for each house.

Redundant or back-up equipment for systems and equipment listed in Sentence 9.36.5.4.(1). is permitted to be excluded, provided it is equipped with controls and is not required to meet to space-conditioning load of house.

sentence.text: The emissions factors associated with the use of energy utilities consumed by the building's systems shall be

clause.text: 0.011 kg CO2e/kWh for electricity,
0.180 kg CO2e/kWh for natural gas,
0.2155 kg CO2e/kWh for propane,
0.2579 kg CO2e/kWh for light fuel oil (standard),
0.2689 kg CO2e/kWh for heavy fuel oil,
0.2581 kg CO2e/kWh for diesel fuel (standard), and
0.0786 kg CO2e/kWh for wood fuel.

sentence.text: Calculations for greenhouse gas emissions compliance shall include:

clause.text: the energy sources for building systems described in Sentence 9.36.6.2.(1) using the emissions factors established in Sentence 9.37.1.3.(2), and
modeled in accordance with Article 9.36.6.4.

Insert new Subsection 9.36.6 Energy Step Code with Article 9.36.6.1 Application

sentence.text: Where a building contains more than one dwelling unit, the requirements of this Subsection shall apply to the energy performance of the building and not to individual dwelling units.

This Subsection is concerned with determining compliance with one of the energy performance Steps through modeling the energy performance of components, systems and assemblies that are installed in buildings and houses with or without a secondary suite, described in .

For the purpose of this Subsection, the term "mechanical energy use intensity" shall mean a metric of the energy used over a year by the building, estimated by using an energy model in accordance with , normalized per square metre of floor area of conditioned space and expressed in kWh/(m2·year), for all of the following combined:

clause.text: space-heating equipment,
space-cooling equipment,
fans,
service water heating equipment,
pumps, and
auxiliary HVAC equipment (see Note A-9.36.6.2.(1)(f)).

sentence.text: Notwithstanding , for the purpose of this Subsection, the term "house" shall mean all houses, with or without a secondary suite, that

clause.text: have HVAC systems that serve only the house, only the secondary suite, or both the house and the secondary suite including their common spaces,
have service water heating systems that serve only the house, only the secondary suite, or both the house and the secondary suite including their common spaces, and
except for common spaces in the house with the secondary suite, do not share common spaces intended for occupancy with other dwelling units or houses.

sentence.text: For the purpose of this Subsection, the term "thermal energy demand intensity" shall mean a metric of the annual heating required by the building for space conditioning and for conditioning of ventilation air, estimated by using an energy model in accordance with , normalized per square metre of floor area of conditioned space and expressed in kWh/(m2·year), taking into account all of the following:

clause.text: thermal transmittance of above-ground walls and roof-ceiling assemblies,
thermal transmittance of floors and walls in contact with the ground, or with space that is not conditioned space,
thermal transmittance and solar heat gain of windows, doors and skylights,
air leakage through the air barrier system,
internal heat gains from occupants and equipment, and
heat recovery from exhaust ventilation.

sentence.text: For the purpose of this Subsection, the term "Step" shall mean a Step referred to in Tables 9.36.6.3.-A to 9.36.6.3.-H.
Buildings conforming to the requirements of any of Steps 3 to 5 shall be designed and constructed to conform to the applicable energy performance requirements in

clause.text: Table 9.36.6.3.-G, or
a combination thereof.

sentence.text: Except as permitted by ,

clause.text: energy performance shall be calculated in conformance with and , and
